title: Bathing-water per-sample microbiology (E. coli / intestinal enterococci counts + discountable flag) for near-cutoff sites, England 2015-2024
vintage: Per-sample in-season microbiology results for the rolling 4-year windows (2012-2015) of every 2015 site-year classified Poor or Sufficient — the two bands adjacent to the action-triggering Poor/Sufficient boundary, scoped to the decision cohort the shipped mark uses. One row per sample: E. coli and intestinal enterococci counts (with detection-limit qualifiers) and the discountable flag (whether the EA may disregard the sample as an abnormal short-term-pollution / extreme-rainfall result). Re-runnable for further decision cohorts.
notes: Backs the bathing-water abnormal-sample-exclusion sensitivity check (the seam-specific manipulation analogue): re-including discountable samples and re-deriving the log-normal 90th-percentile margin near the cutoff. Assembled once by scripts/bathingwater_harvest.py from the in-season sample-assessment endpoints; hash-pinned and mirrored to object storage. Scoped to near-cutoff bands to keep the harvest bounded; the standard battery and headline effect use only the compliance file.
